# Compatibility Checklist: UC-29 Auto-pick Compatible Components

**Purpose**: Validate the clarity, completeness, and consistency of requirements for compatibility-aware lecture, lab, tutorial, and shared-component schedule generation
**Created**: 2026-03-11
**Feature**: [spec.md](/home/thaia/ECE493Group19/specs/029-compatible-components/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are requirements defined for how compatibility rules are retrieved before schedule generation begins? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001]
- [x] CHK002 Are requirements explicit for courses with only some required component types, not only lecture/lab/tutorial triples? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003]
- [x] CHK003 Are all major generation outcomes documented, including valid schedules, no-compatible-combination blocking, missing-rule blocking, shared-component handling, and generation failure? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK004 Does the spec define whether all valid compatible combinations should be considered or only a subset? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005]
- [x] CHK005 Are cross-listed or shared-component scenarios explicitly included in the feature scope rather than implied only by edge cases? [Completeness, Spec §US3]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is “compatible component combination” defined clearly enough to know what must be true before a schedule option is returned? [Clarity, Spec §Key Entities]
- [x] CHK007 Is the policy for missing or inconsistent linkage rules unambiguous about whether generation is blocked or flagged? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007]
- [x] CHK008 Is the rule for shared compatible components specific enough to prevent multiple reasonable display or counting interpretations? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008]
- [x] CHK009 Is the phrase “makes the chosen compatible component pairings clear” quantified enough to be testable without interpretation drift? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-011]
- [x] CHK010 Is “current term” or “selected term” used consistently enough to avoid ambiguity about the data scope of compatibility checks? [Consistency, Spec §FR-001]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Do the user stories, edge cases, and functional requirements align on blocking generation when compatibility cannot be verified? [Consistency, Spec §US2, Spec §FR-007]
- [x] CHK012 Are the shared-component acceptance scenario and the shared-component functional requirement fully aligned on single representation and dual satisfaction semantics? [Consistency, Spec §US3, Spec §FR-008]
- [x] CHK013 Do the failure-handling requirements align between the use-case extension, user story 3, and `FR-009`? [Consistency, Spec §US3, Spec §FR-009]
- [x] CHK014 Are cross-term isolation requirements consistent between assumptions, dependencies, and `FR-010`? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Can each success criterion be objectively verified without inferring hidden scheduling behavior? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001]
- [x] CHK016 Are the acceptance scenarios sufficient to distinguish “no compatible combination exists” from “compatibility could not be verified”? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §US2]
- [x] CHK017 Is the shared-component success criterion measurable enough to detect both duplication and incorrect dual-satisfaction handling? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK018 Are primary, alternate, exception, and recovery-style outcomes represented across the user stories and requirements? [Coverage, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing]
- [x] CHK019 Are requirements defined for mixed-course requests where one course blocks generation and others would otherwise be valid? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK020 Does the spec define how compatibility requirements apply when multiple courses each have several valid compatible combinations? [Coverage, Spec §FR-005]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK021 Are boundary conditions for missing component types, missing rules, contradictory rules, and shared components all addressed in the requirements? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK022 Are requirements explicit about returning no partial schedules after generation failure or unverifiable compatibility? [Coverage, Spec §FR-007]
- [x] CHK023 Are rollback or consistency expectations defined when generation fails after some combinations have already been evaluated? [Gap]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK024 Are performance requirements quantified for both successful generation and blocked-generation explanations? [Clarity, Spec §NFR-001]
- [x] CHK025 Are plain-language messaging requirements specific enough to cover no-compatible-combination, missing-rule, shared-component, and failure outcomes? [Coverage, Spec §NFR-002]
- [x] CHK026 Are privacy requirements explicit about using only the authenticated student's request and term context during generation? [Coverage, Spec §NFR-003]
- [x] CHK027 Can the non-functional success target be measured consistently across success, block, and failure paths? [Measurability, Spec §NFR-004]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK028 Are assumptions about catalog-provided linkage rules validated against the feature scope and edge cases? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK029 Are dependencies on schedule-generator consumption of compatible combinations documented clearly enough to support downstream planning? [Dependency, Spec §Dependencies]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK030 Does the spec avoid ambiguous uses of “correct,” “compatible,” and “clear” unless operationally defined in context? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-002]
- [x] CHK031 Is the source of truth for compatibility linkage rules intentionally bounded, or is it still underspecified between catalog and timetable sources? [Gap, Spec §Dependencies]

## Notes

- This checklist assumes standard reviewer depth for PR or spec review.
- Focus areas selected: compatibility-rule completeness, blocked-generation policy, and shared-component clarity.
- Actor/timing: reviewer during pre-implementation requirements review.
- Tightened requirement areas: authoritative linkage-rule source, multi-course valid-combination coverage, visible section-level pairing details, and no partial-result exposure after generation failure.
